🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 19 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup adjacent to insurtech, building digital underwriting or embedded insurance distribution software for regulated brokers.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European seed-stage regulated-intermediary fintech startup similar to Zelros or Seyna automating compliance or policy management for insurance or wealth firms.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Fintecture (anchor)
2. Trustpair (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 4 companies → evaluated: 1 for Profund VC

Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 21 → no URL: -3 → passed to LLM: 17

Nscale10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: Nscale is an AI infrastructure company providing GPU compute and data centers, not a B2B software product for workflow automation or regulated intermediaries as required by the thesis.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/nscale-raises-790m
ProcurePro10NO MATCHThe company's core business is construction procurement, not AI-native workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech for French companies. The company is headquartered in Australia.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/procurepro-raises-
Optro0NO MATCHThe company is headquartered in the United States, failing the geographical requirement of the thesis which specifies 'French SMEs or mid-market companies' and 'French regulated intermediaries'.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/optro-acquires-mid
Cowboy Space Corporation0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. Cowboy Space Corporation is building orbital infrastructure and space-based AI compute, which is a hardware and space-tech business, not B2B software (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology).https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/cowboy-space-corpo
Lyrie0NO MATCHSTEP B fails because Lyrie provides cybersecurity for AI agents and operates globally from Dubai, not an AI-native workflow automation product for French SMEs, nor regulated-intermediary fintech for France.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/lyrie-completes-2-
VoltaGrid0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. VoltaGrid provides advanced energy management and generation solutions, including hardware (portable power, CNG fuel supply) and services for data centers and industrial customers. This is not a B2B software product (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology) and does not align with 'ai_workflow_automation' or 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angles.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/voltagrid-announce
Frame Security0NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed because the company is headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, which is outside Europe. The 'AI workflow automation' angle specifically requires companies headquartered in Europe.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/frame-security-rai
Ripple20NO MATCHRipple provides blockchain-based enterprise solutions for traditional and digital finance, which is a B2B software product, satisfying STEP A. InMobi10NO MATCHThe company, InMobi, is an advertising technology platform focused on app visibility and user acquisition. Driive10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A, B) — Driive is a B2B SaaS for home service trades in the US, which does not align with the AI workflow automation's required customer (French SMEs/mid-market companies) or the regulated intermediary fintech angle (French financial intermediaries).https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/driive-closes-pre-
Coddy0NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: Coddy is an EdTech platform for individual learners, making it a B2C offering, not a B2B software product for professional or institutional buyers.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/coddy-raises-10m-i
Parker10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails - Parker is a diversified manufacturer of motion and control technologies and systems, not a B2B software product company.https://www.parker.com/us/en/about-parker.html
Config0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The provided URL, description, and company name 'Config' refer to Microsoft Windows system settings documentation, not an independent company with a product.https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/config

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 3 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

Week 20 → angle: ai_workflow_automationParis | Machine Learning | 11-50 employees

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 187 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
3 MATCH · 90 NO MATCH · 3% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 19 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
2. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 8d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 9d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 10d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 11d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 12d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 13d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 14d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 15d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 16d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 17d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 18d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 19d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 20d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 21d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 22d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 23d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 24d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 25d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 26d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 27d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
★ TODAY“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 1d — Wed May 13“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 2d — Thu May 14“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 3d — Fri May 15“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 4d — Sat May 16“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 5d — Sun May 17“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 6d — Mon May 18“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 7d — Tue May 19“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Comtravo (anchor)
2. Etta (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 4 companies → evaluated: 1 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

InMobi10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. InMobi and MobileAction operate in the mobile advertising and app store optimization (ASO) market, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. The company's focus is on helping app developers and marketers optimize app visibility and acquire users, not on automating business travel processes.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/inmobi-acquires-mo
Driive10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company provides an AI-native booking and scheduling platform for home service trades (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical) and is not involved in European corporate travel technology.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/driive-closes-pre-
Coddy10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. Coddy is an EdTech platform focused on teaching coding to individual consumers, not a B2B corporate travel technology company.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/coddy-raises-10m-i
Parker10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The provided description largely consists of links and generic marketing fragments, not detailing the core business or product offerings sufficiently to determine fit with corporate travel tech.https://www.parker.com/us/en/about-parker.html
Config0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The provided URL points to a Microsoft support page related to Windows system settings, not a company called 'Config' providing corporate travel tech.https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/config

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 3 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 187 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

🟪 90/100 CBT Suite
https://cbtsuite.com
Summary: - What the company does: CBT Suite offers an all-in-one, AI-powered travel management platform for traditional travel management companies (TMCs). It provides end-to-end travel management tools, including an agent booking platform, self-booking tool, expense management, …
📍 WHERE FOUND: Exa Semantic — query: "Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS"
🤖 WHY IT MATCHES:
The company's core business is an AI-powered travel management platform for TMCs, aligning with the European corporate travel technology domain (STEP A). It specifically fits the 'tmc_workflow_platform' angle by providing an all-in-one platform for TMCs to manage corporate travel, automate operations, and improve efficiency (STEP B).
🟪 90/100 FCM Travel France
https://fcmtravel.com/fr
📍 WHERE FOUND: Exa Semantic — query: "Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS"
🤖 WHY IT MATCHES:
The company's primary business per its description as 'FCM Travel France' and its URL 'fcmtravel.com/fr' clearly indicates it is a large Travel Management Company operating in Europe, providing corporate travel services. This aligns with the 'tmc_workflow_platform' angle, as it is a TMC, and the thesis aims at consolidation within this sector.
🟪 90/100 amnis
https://amnistreasury.com
Summary: - What the company does: amnis is a financial services company offering an all-in-one banking solution for businesses. Core features include a CH-IBAN business account for local collections, free local payments/collections in the UK, US, CH, and EU, multi-currency busin…

The company's core business is financial services specifically for businesses, including multi-currency debit cards for expense management and cross-border payments, aligning well with the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle by providing SaaS/fintech infrastructure for corporate travel payments and expense management in Europe.
